$comment: Model 3 (2026-06-09): PLATFORM-NEUTRAL descriptor. `library` is a per-object-format map; the active target's format selects its runtime image at compile_pipeline resolution. The `fseek` offset (param[1]) and `ftell` result are the C `long` type, encoded here in the LP64 (i64) form correct for the runnable elf/macho targets. FC3 c1: the Windows LLP64 (i32) form for these 2 is now declared per-symbol via signatureByDataModel (the active format dataModel selects it; D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH). ★ c82 (D-FFI-SHELL-STDIO-SEMANTIC-SURFACE): (1) `typedefs` ships FILE as the opaque empty-named struct `struct "FILE" {}` — the EXACT TypeId every signature below already spells (interner-deduped), the dirent.json DIR precedent: `FILE *f` resolves in type position (+ the parse oracle steers block-scope `FILE *in;` declarations), member access on it fails loud. size_t rides too — C89 7.19.1 REQUIRES stdio.h to provide size_t + NULL without stddef.h (sqlite's shell.c never includes stddef.h); u64 = the stddef.json width (LP64+LLP64 agree), first-wins dedup makes double-provision with stddef/string/stdlib/time moot. (2) the printf family ships as VARIADIC externs (`fn(..., ...)`) — the c14c fcntl/ioctl mechanism; the README's variadic-exclusion note predates c14c and is updated. vfprintf's third param is spelled `va_list` — resolved by the reader's named-type bindings (threaded from the semantic tier's per-CC va_list mint, D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VA-LIST-TYPE) so the descriptor stays arch-NEUTRAL while the param lands the ABI-exact type (SysV: __va_list_tag[1]; AAPCS64: the 32B __va_list struct; Win64: char*) — byte-identical to a user-written `extern int vfprintf(FILE*, const char*, va_list);`. (3) popen/pclose/fileno are NOT shipped: shell.c self-declares popen/pclose at file scope (goal-2: the user decl wins + FF5 binds the default libc) and fileno is _WIN32-only there; shipping them un-consumed would be over-ship — additive when a real consumer lands. (4) stdout/stderr/stdin extern OBJECTS are NOT shipped: a library DATA import needs the extern-data link model (COPY reloc vs GOT-load) — the c82 §B fork (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT); the MIR tier lowers ExternGlobal since c82 but the link tier fail-louds on a surviving data import until the model is user-decided.
